title: NASA Rover Finds Hidden River Under Mars
summary: NASA's Perseverance rover is exploring Mars to look for signs of ancient life. It landed in Jezero Crater, which used to be a lake. The rover has a special radar called RIMFAX that can see underground. Recently, it discovered an old river delta buried deep under the surface. A river delta is a fan-shaped area where a river drops sand and rocks into a lake. This hidden delta might have been a good place for tiny life forms, called microbes, to live long ago. Scientists are excited because finding water signs on Mars helps us learn if life ever existed there. The rover keeps driving and using its radar to study the rocks and layers underground, giving us new clues about Mars' wet past.

keywords.explanation: a robot car that explores other planets
a tool that uses waves to see things hidden underground
a fan-shaped area where a river drops sand into a lake
a big hole in the ground, often from an impact
tiny living things too small to see without a microscope

questions.question: What tiny life forms might have lived in the ancient delta?
What shape is a river delta often described as?

background_read: Mars is a planet in our solar system that scientists study to see if it ever had life. Long ago, Mars might have had water like Earth, with rivers and lakes. NASA sends rovers like Perseverance to explore Mars and take pictures and measurements. A river delta forms when a river flows into a lake and drops sand and rocks, creating layers. Finding an old delta on Mars suggests water was there, which is important because water is needed for life as we know it. This discovery helps us understand Mars' history and if tiny organisms could have lived there billions of years ago.
Article_Structure: WHO: NASA's Perseverance rover and scientists like Emily L. Cardarelli
WHAT: Found an ancient river delta buried underground on Mars using radar
WHERE: In Jezero Crater on Mars
WHY: To search for signs of ancient life and learn about Mars' wet past

perspectives.description: The finding shows Mars had flowing water long ago, which could have supported life.
